sourceMovie.title: Dreamgirls
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dreamgirls works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and music blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Rocketman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dreamgirls clicked for you, Rocketman (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and music, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: CODA
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dreamgirls clicked for you, CODA (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and music, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dreamgirls?
faq.answer: Try Rocketman, CODA, Judy—each shares DNA with Dreamgirls while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dreamgirls?
faq.answer: Dreamgirls (2006) is best described as Drama, Music. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dreamgirls worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
